item.description: The Virginia Supreme Court blocked a new Democratic-drawn congressional map from taking effect weeks after the voters narrowly approved the plan in a statewide vote. NBC News' Jane Timm explains how this delivers a major boost to Republicans as they defend their narrow House majority in the midterm elections.

media:content.media:title: Virginia Supreme Court blocks congressional map favoring Democrats
media:content.media:description: The Virginia Supreme Court blocked a new Democratic-drawn congressional map from taking effect weeks after the voters narrowly approved the plan in a statewide vote. NBC News' Jane Timm explains how this delivers a major boost to Republicans as they defend their narrow House majority in the midterm elections.

item.title: Virginia Supreme Court blocks congressional map favoring Democrats

item.dc:creator: Jane C. Timm
item.description: The Virginia Supreme Court on Friday blocked a new Democratic-drawn congressional map from taking effect, delivering a major boost for Republicans as they defend their narrow House majority in the midterm elections.

media:content.media:text: Signs promoting Virginia's redistricting referendum vote
media:content.media:description: Signs promoting Virginia's redistricting referendum vote in Norfolk on April 15, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Image: A sign urges early voters to vote no on the Virginia redistricting referendum
media:content.media:description: A sign urges early voters to vote no on the Virginia redistricting referendum at the Ellen M. Bozman Government Center in Arlington on March 31.

item.title: Virginia Supreme Court blocks Democratic-drawn map voters approved

item.dc:creator: Jane C. Timm
item.description: Republican lawmakers passed a bill that would set up special primary elections if they are allowed to implement new maps after the U.S. Supreme Court’s redistricting ruling.

media:content.media:description: Demonstrators protests against Alabama Republicans' redistricting plan.

item.title: Alabama takes step toward a new congressional map while awaiting court action

item.dc:creator: Jane C. Timm
item.description: Tennessee’s Republican-led Legislature passed a new congressional map dividing up the state’s lone majority-Black district, swiftly responding to the U.S.

media:content.media:description: Protesters yell as state troopers begin to clear the house gallery under the orders of House Speaker Cameron Sexton on the third day of the special session to redraw congressional maps for Tennessee at the State Capitol in Nashville, Tenn., on May 7, 2026.
Democratic representatives walk off the House floor as Republicans pass their redistricting plan.
Protesters line the halls at the State Capitol.
Democratic Rep. Justin Jones walks out of the House chamber and burns a photo of the Confederate flag.

item.title: Tennessee Republicans pass map dividing up state’s lone majority-Black district

item.description: President Donald Trump says the fragile ceasefire is still in effect even as attacks escalate between the United States and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. Central Command said Iran fired missiles and launched drones at three American destroyers, calling the attacks “unprovoked.” They also said U.S. forces responded with self defense strikes targeting military facilities and drone launch sites in Iran. Iran says it was the U.S. who fired first and that its attacks were defensive. NBC’s

media:content.media:title: Trump Says US and Iran Ceasefire Stills Holds Amid New Attacks
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump says the fragile ceasefire is still in effect even as attacks escalate between the United States and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. Central Command said Iran fired missiles and launched drones at three American destroyers, calling the attacks “unprovoked.” They also said U.S. forces responded with self defense strikes targeting military facilities and drone launch sites in Iran. Iran says it was the U.S. who fired first and that its attacks were defensive. NBC’s Matt Bradley reports and Kristen Welker, moderator of Meet the Press, joins TODAY with analysis.

item.title: Trump Says US and Iran Ceasefire Stills Holds Amid New Attacks

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
item.description: The Trump administration is violating ABC’s free speech rights with a regulatory action focused on “The View,” the broadcast network argued in an FCC filing.

media:content.media:text: Marlon Wayans is a guest on "The View" Sep. 17, 2025.
media:content.media:description: Marlon Wayans is a guest on "The View" on Sept. 17, 2025.

media:content.media:text: Federal Communications Commission Holds Open Meeting In Washington, DC
media:content.media:description: Marlon Wayans is a guest on "The View" on Sept. 17, 2025.

item.title: ABC accuses Trump administration of violating free speech rights

item.description: The ship at the center of the deadly hantavirus outbreak is heading to the Canary Islands as health officials scramble to track passengers who disembarked from the ship early. It comes as new suspected cases are popping in multiple countries, including in the United Kingdom and Singapore. NBC’s Daniele Hamamdjian reports for TODAY from Tenerife.

media:content.media:title: Officials Race to Track Hantavirus After Outbreak on Cruise Ship
media:content.media:description: The ship at the center of the deadly hantavirus outbreak is heading to the Canary Islands as health officials scramble to track passengers who disembarked from the ship early. It comes as new suspected cases are popping in multiple countries, including in the United Kingdom and Singapore. NBC’s Daniele Hamamdjian reports for TODAY from Tenerife.

item.title: Officials Race to Track Hantavirus After Outbreak on Cruise Ship

item.dc:creator: Corky Siemaszko
Denise Chow
Mosheh Gains
Laura Strickler
item.description: The Pentagon on Friday began releasing “never-before-seen” files relating to unidentified anomalous phenomena — previously and more infamously known as unidentified flying objects, or UFOs — that the government has been holding onto for decades

media:content.media:text: The U.S. Indo-Pacific Command submitted a report of an unidentified anomalous phenomenon consisting of nine seconds of video footage from an infrared sensor aboard a U.S. military platform in 2024.
media:content.media:description: The U.S. Indo-Pacific Command submitted a report of an unidentified anomalous phenomenon consisting of one minute and 39 seconds of video footage from an infrared sensor aboard a U.S. military platform in 2024.

media:content.media:text: A photo of the landing site of Apollo 12 features a highlighted area of interest slightly above the horizon in which unidentified phenomena are visible.
media:content.media:description: The U.S. Indo-Pacific Command submitted a report of an unidentified anomalous phenomenon consisting of one minute and 39 seconds of video footage from an infrared sensor aboard a U.S. military platform in 2024.

media:content.media:text: A photo from the Apollo 17 mission in December 1972 contains three “dots” in a triangular formation in the lower right quadrant of the lunar sky that is clearly visible upon magnification of the image.
media:content.media:description: The U.S. Indo-Pacific Command submitted a report of an unidentified anomalous phenomenon consisting of one minute and 39 seconds of video footage from an infrared sensor aboard a U.S. military platform in 2024.

media:content.media:text: The U.S. Indo-Pacific Command submitted a report of an unidentified anomalous phenomenon to the All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) consisting of nine seconds of video footage from an infrared sensor aboard a U.S. military platform in 2024.
media:content.media:description: The U.S. Indo-Pacific Command submitted a report of an unidentified anomalous phenomenon consisting of one minute and 39 seconds of video footage from an infrared sensor aboard a U.S. military platform in 2024.

media:content.media:text: The U.S. Northern Command submitted a report of an unidentified anomalous phenomenon consisting of 21 seconds of video footage from an infrared sensor aboard a U.S. military platform in 2024.
media:content.media:description: The U.S. Indo-Pacific Command submitted a report of an unidentified anomalous phenomenon consisting of one minute and 39 seconds of video footage from an infrared sensor aboard a U.S. military platform in 2024.

item.title: Pentagon releases declassified UFO files including images held by government for decades

item.dc:creator: David K. Li
Griffin Eckstein
Brittany Kubicko
item.description: Schools across America continued to flounder Friday after a massive cyber attack that’s thrown final exam schedules and classroom activities into chaos.

media:content.media:text: Students relax on the front steps of Low Memorial Library at Columbia University in New York in 2023.
media:content.media:description: Columbia University was one of the schools affected by the Canvas software hack.

item.title: How a massive hack on school software disrupted classes across America

item.description: NBC News' Rebecca Keegan joins "Here's the Scoop" to talk about how Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni's case raises questions of just how much public perception could be manipulated, which Lively's team has alleged that Baldoni has done to target her reputation.

media:content.media:title: Questions remain unanswered after Baldoni-Lively settlement
media:content.media:description: NBC News' Rebecca Keegan joins "Here's the Scoop" to talk about how Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni's case raises questions of just how much public perception could be manipulated, which Lively's team has alleged that Baldoni has done to target her reputation.

item.title: Questions remain unanswered after Baldoni-Lively settlement

item.description: NBC News' Courtney Kube joins "Here's the Scoop" to talk about the newly released UFO files by the Pentagon — and what they contained.

media:content.media:title: What do the Pentagon's UFO files contain?
media:content.media:description: NBC News' Courtney Kube joins "Here's the Scoop" to talk about the newly released UFO files by the Pentagon — and what they contained.

item.title: What do the Pentagon's UFO files contain?

item.description: After Tennessee Republicans passed a new congressional map that broke up the state's only majority-Black district, NAACP General Counsel Kristen Clarke says the justification given by Tennessee lawmakers for the redistricting "lacks credibility."

media:content.media:title: NAACP: Alito gave lawmakers 'playbook' to gerrymander
media:content.media:description: After Tennessee Republicans passed a new congressional map that broke up the state's only majority-Black district, NAACP General Counsel Kristen Clarke says the justification given by Tennessee lawmakers for the redistricting "lacks credibility."

item.title: NAACP: Alito gave lawmakers 'playbook' to gerrymander

item.description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

media:content.media:title: The evolution of album rollouts
media:content.media:description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

item.title: The evolution of album rollouts
